slides.title: The part of the grain that *keeps you steady*.
slides.sub: Intact whole grains carry a fiber-rich bran and germ that refining strips away. That difference matters more than you think.

slides.title: Intact, not refined
slides.body: A whole grain keeps its bran and germ. Refining removes both, leaving mostly fast starch and *very little fiber*.

slides.title: Fiber slows the rise
slides.body: The bran and germ slow how quickly glucose enters your blood, which *can support* steadier energy and gentler blood sugar.

slides.title: Linked to longer life
slides.body: Higher whole grain intake is linked to lower all-cause mortality in large studies. The fiber and nutrients *seem to matter most*.

slides.title: Trade up your staples
slides.body: Choose oats, brown rice, barley or true whole grain bread. Look for *whole grain listed first* on the label.

slides.title: Color can mislead
slides.body: Brown does not mean whole. Many loaves are refined flour with coloring, so *check the ingredient list* before you trust it.

slides.title: Make every grain *count*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs your food alongside sleep, movement and steps, turning small swaps like this into one daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The difference between white bread and true whole grain is the part most loaves throw away: the fiber-rich bran and germ. 🌾

They slow how fast glucose hits your blood, and higher whole grain intake is linked to lower all-cause mortality. Check the label, not the color. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns small food swaps into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Whole Grains Beat Refined for Longevity
